purpose: Compare nearby local businesses or services — groomers, dentists, studios, repair shops — grouped by neighborhood or category.
primaryUserGoal: Pick a local business that fits the user's needs without needing a separate search tool.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User asks for nearby options outside the restaurant/hotel flow: 'best dog groomers near me', 'dentists nearby', 'yoga studios in my area'.
goodFor: Any local shortlist outside food + lodging
supports: Grouped list by neighborhood or subcategory
Detail panel with hours, contact, price chips, photo
Transition to event-planner when user wants to book or plan around the choice
idealDataShape: Filter chips the user cares about
Per-option: hours, address, price tier, contact

populationInstructions.summary: Start with filter chips for the user's constraints. Group 4-8 local providers by neighborhood on the left. Populate the detail-panel for the initially-selected provider with hours, address, contact, price, and a photo query.
steps: Parse the category (groomer, salon, studio, etc.) and location.
Emit filter chips for category, distance, price, and any user-specified constraint.
Group 4-8 providers by neighborhood. Tone-code the top-pick group with success.
Detail panel for the highest-match provider: hours, address, phone, price chips, 2-3 bullets.
Include an image descriptor (leadingImage or detail image) with query like '<business name>' or '<category> storefront'.
Keep subtitles short — cuisine/vibe/price tier, not full description.
guardrails: Don't invent phone numbers or addresses.
If unsure on hours, say 'call to confirm'.
Avoid duplicating the restaurant/hotel templates — this is for everything else.

patchPrefer: Use set_filter_chips when the user refines constraints
Use upsert_groups to add or refresh providers
Use set_detail when the user selects a different option
replaceTriggers: User changes city or category entirely
